articles.summary: Khamenei’s sons attend funeral prayers in Tehran The Associated Press reported that Ali Khamenei’s sons Masoud, Meysam and Mostafa attended funeral prayers for the late Iranian supreme leader in Tehran on Sunday. The ceremony was also attended by Ahmad Vahidi, commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. Funeral ceremonies continue for Khamenei, senior officials join
articles.summary: Funeral ceremonies continue for Khamenei, senior officials join Funeral prayers were held for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei on Sunday at Tehran’s Grand Mosalla complex, marking the second day of ceremonies that have drawn large crowds to the capital. President Masoud Pezeshkian, parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and other senior officials attended the prayers, which were led by prominent Shia cleric Jafar Sobhani. Khamenei’s coffin, draped in the Iranian flag, was placed alongside the coffins of four relatives who were also killed in the 28 February strikes by US and Israel

articles.summary: Video: Israeli settlers storm Hebron's Old City under protection of Israeli forces Illegal Israeli settlers entered Hebron's Old City under the protection of Israeli forces, with footage showing heavily armed soldiers clearing Palestinian residents from streets and public areas to allow the settlers to move through the area. Videos from the scene show settlers roaming the Old City while Israeli forces imposed restrictions on local Palestinian movement. The incident comes amid growing concern over expanding illegal settler activity and tighter Israeli control in Hebron's historic centre. Illega

articles.title: Pakistan to host new round of US-Iran talks, report says
articles.summary: Pakistan to host new round of US-Iran talks, report says Pakistan is expected to host a new round of talks between the United States and Iran on July 11, according to Saudi state-owned broadcaster Al Arabiya, which cited unnamed sources. The report said the discussions are expected to focus on US sanctions on Iran, frozen Iranian assets and Tehran’s nuclear programme. Al Arabiya added that the composition of the Iranian delegation has not yet been finalised and will be decided after the funeral of former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.

articles.summary: Two Palestinians killed, several injured in Israeli drone strikes in Gaza Palestinian news agency WAFA reported that a Palestinian was killed after an Israeli drone struck a group of people near the Asqula junction, east of Gaza City. Another strike killed one more Palestinian and injured several others, some critically, in an Israeli drone attack near the Abu Sharkh roundabout, west of Jabalia refugee camp in the northern Gaza Strip, WAFA reported. articles.summary: More than 140 Palestinian citizens of Israel killed this year More than 140 Palestinian citizens of Israel have been killed so far this year, a 12 percent increase from the same period in 2025, according to data cited by AFP from the Abraham Initiatives, an organisation that promotes coexistence between Jewish and Arab communities. The deaths have been linked largely to criminal gangs, family feuds, widespread access to firearms and what many community members describe as inadequate police enforcement. articles.summary: OPEC+ tipped to raise quotas again as Middle East calms Seven OPEC+ members are likely to raise oil production quotas on Sunday as Gulf countries are reeling from the Middle East war, analysts told AFP. Saudi Arabia, Russia and five other members of the enlarged Organisation of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC+) are meeting online on Sunday to discuss quotas for August. articles.title: Ship-tracking firm says four ships changed course in Hormuz
articles.summary: Ship-tracking firm says four ships changed course in Hormuz Four vessels attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz via the route along Oman’s coast have altered course, ship-tracing firm Windward said. In a post on X, Windward said two of the vessels reversed course and the other two diverted to a central corridor. Iran opposes ships using the southern route through Oman’s territorial waters and has previously attacked at least two vessels travelling that corridor.  The attacks triggered retaliatory attacks on Iranian military assets by the US in and around Hormuz. articles.title: Trump says Netanyahu ‘knows who the boss is’
articles.summary: Trump says Netanyahu ‘knows who the boss is’ US President Donald Trump said he and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu “get along very good” and added that the Israeli leader “knows who the boss is” in a phone interview with Axios. Trump also confirmed that he expects to meet Netanyahu soon after the Nato summit in Turkey, saying the Israeli prime minister had requested a meeting at the White House. articles.title: 'Expected him to be unpopular': Trump shocked by Khamenei funeral turnout
articles.summary: 'Expected him to be unpopular': Trump shocked by Khamenei funeral turnout US President Donald Trump has said he was "shocked" by the massive turnout at the funeral of Iran’s assassinated supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, saying he had expected the late leader to be widely unpopular. Trump told Axios, "I was shocked. I thought people hated him.”  "Maybe it's fake tears," he said, after seeing mourners crying during the funeral ceremonies. articles.title: Pezeshkian criticises failure to stop Israeli attacks, calls for greater Muslim unity
articles.summary: Pezeshkian criticises failure to stop Israeli attacks, calls for greater Muslim unity Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian criticised what he described as the international community’s failure to prevent Israeli attacks across the region, blaming the inaction for continued instability across the Middle East. Speaking at a ceremony commemorating the late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, Pezeshkian said Iran’s new leadership carried a heavy responsibility to strengthen Islamic unity and expand cooperation among Muslim nations in response to regional challenges.
